name: Type 1 Diabetes - Elevated HbA1c Lab Query
description: Tests ability to find type 1 diabetes patients via elevated HbA1c. The diagnostic threshold for diabetes (T1 or T2) is HbA1c ≥ 6.5%. T1D typically presents with much higher HbA1c at diagnosis (often >10%) due to absolute insulin deficiency. This test case uses ≥ 6.5% to capture both well-controlled and poorly-controlled T1D patients. Same LOINC and threshold as T2D — distinction from T2D is in the diagnosis codes, not labs.
prompt: Find laboratory observations for hemoglobin A1c results at or above 6.5 percent, indicating diabetes mellitus.

prompts.broad: Find Observation resources for hemoglobin A1c results where the value is greater than or equal to 6.5 percent. HbA1c ≥ 6.5% meets ADA diagnostic criteria for diabetes mellitus (Type 1 or Type 2).
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources for elevated HbA1c. Use LOINC 4548-4 (Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood). Apply a value-quantity filter with the ge prefix at threshold 6.5, unit percent. The query should look like: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|4548-4&value-quantity=ge6.5. Note this is the ADA diabetes diagnostic threshold — same as T2D. Distinction between T1D and T2D requires diagnosis codes, not labs alone.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated HbA1c (Paths A and D). Will catch both T1D and T2D patients — distinguishing requires diagnosis codes.
